dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, everything's falling apart. The settlement found out what we've been doing.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} saw you heading toward the {enemy_type} camp and told the whole settlement. Now the guards are organizing a raid to wipe them out, children and all. They're marching at dawn. We have until then to gather enough {quest_item} to convince the {enemy_type} to relocate somewhere safe, or there's going to be a slaughter on both sides. The {enemy_type} won't leave without supplies for the journey.
dialogueChunks.outro: I started this. If blood gets spilled because I stayed silent, I'll never forgive myself.

playerResponses.text: Then we move fast. I'll get the supplies before dawn.
You should have spoken up sooner. But I'll try to stop this.
You chose the wrong side. I'm staying out of this.

dialogueChunks.intro: This is worse than I feared, {player_name}. Much worse.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} took your visit as a sign of alliance. Now they're coming to {location} expecting friendship, and the guards have no idea they're coming peacefully. They'll see armed {enemy_type} approaching the gates and assume it's an attack. You need to intercept them {location_hint} and convince them to turn back before they walk into a bloodbath. Take {quest_item} as a gesture of good faith.
dialogueChunks.outro: Two groups who could live in peace are about to destroy each other over a misunderstanding. You might be the only person who can prevent it.

playerResponses.text: I'll intercept them. Nobody dies today.
Caught between two sides and trusted by neither. Wonderful.
Let them sort it out themselves. This isn't my war.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something terrible has happened at the {enemy_type} camp.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A rogue faction within the {enemy_type} killed their own elder: the one who wanted peace. The hardliners have taken control and they're planning revenge against {location} for generations of hostility. {target_npc} says the remaining moderates fled {location_hint} and they're the only ones who can calm the war party. We need to find them and bring them {quest_item} to prove we still want peace, before the hardliners attack.
dialogueChunks.outro: The window for peace is closing fast. If the moderates lose hope, so does everyone.

playerResponses.text: I'll find the moderates. Peace is worth fighting for.
Their own people killed the peacemaker? This might be hopeless.
Internal politics of monsters isn't my problem.
